framework.name: Cyber Governance Code of Practice
framework.issuing_body: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
framework.scope: Voluntary code of practice setting out actions for directors and board members of medium and large organisations to govern cyber security risks. Aligned with the NCSC Board Toolkit but structured as a governance code.
framework.structure_description: 5 governance actions with supporting guidance for boards: risk management, strategy, people, incident planning, and assurance

controls.title: Risk management
controls.description: Directors should ensure that the most significant cyber risks to the organisation are identified, assessed, and managed. Cyber risk should be integrated into the wider risk management framework and reported to the board regularly.
controls.description_nl: Directors should ensure that the most significant cyber risks to the organisation are identified, assessed, and managed. Cyber risk should be integrated into the wider risk management framework and reported to the board regularly.
controls.implementation_guidance: Integrate cyber risk into the enterprise risk framework. Report cyber risks to the board at least quarterly. Define risk appetite for cyber threats. Use recognised frameworks (CAF, NCSC 10 Steps) for assessment.
controls.verification_guidance: Review cyber risk entries in the enterprise risk register. Verify board reporting frequency. Check risk appetite definition.

controls.title: Identify critical assets and services
controls.description: The board should ensure the organisation has identified its critical digital assets, services, and data that could impact the business if compromised.
controls.description_nl: The board should ensure the organisation has identified its critical digital assets, services, and data that could impact the business if compromised.
controls.implementation_guidance: Catalogue critical digital assets. Assess business impact of their compromise. Report critical assets to the board.
controls.verification_guidance: Review critical asset catalogue. Verify board awareness.

controls.title: Cyber strategy
controls.description: Directors should ensure the organisation has a cyber security strategy that is aligned with the overall business strategy and is resourced appropriately. The strategy should be reviewed annually.
controls.description_nl: Directors should ensure the organisation has a cyber security strategy that is aligned with the overall business strategy and is resourced appropriately. The strategy should be reviewed annually.
controls.implementation_guidance: Develop a cyber security strategy aligned with business objectives. Allocate budget and resources proportionate to risk. Review and update the strategy annually.
controls.verification_guidance: Review cyber strategy document. Verify alignment with business strategy. Check budget allocation.

controls.title: Resource cyber security appropriately
controls.description: The board should ensure cyber security is resourced appropriately with budget, skilled personnel, and management attention proportionate to the identified risks.
controls.description_nl: The board should ensure cyber security is resourced appropriately with budget, skilled personnel, and management attention proportionate to the identified risks.
controls.implementation_guidance: Allocate dedicated cyber security budget. Ensure skilled personnel are available (internal or contracted). Include cyber in board meeting agendas.
controls.verification_guidance: Review budget allocation. Verify staffing levels. Check board agenda coverage.

controls.title: People
controls.description: Directors should ensure that the organisation promotes a positive culture of cyber security awareness and that all staff receive appropriate training. The board itself should have sufficient understanding of cyber risk.
controls.description_nl: Directors should ensure that the organisation promotes a positive culture of cyber security awareness and that all staff receive appropriate training. The board itself should have sufficient understanding of cyber risk.
controls.implementation_guidance: Provide regular cyber awareness training for all staff. Ensure board members receive cyber risk briefings. Develop security champion programmes. Measure and report on security culture.
controls.verification_guidance: Review training completion rates. Check board member briefing schedule. Assess security culture metrics.

controls.title: Incident planning, response and recovery
controls.description: Directors should ensure the organisation has plans to respond to and recover from cyber incidents. Plans should be tested regularly. The board should understand its role during a major incident.
controls.description_nl: Directors should ensure the organisation has plans to respond to and recover from cyber incidents. Plans should be tested regularly. The board should understand its role during a major incident.
controls.implementation_guidance: Maintain a tested incident response plan. Define the board communication role during incidents. Conduct board-level exercises annually. Include regulatory notification procedures.
controls.verification_guidance: Review incident response plan. Verify board exercise participation. Check regulatory notification procedures.

controls.title: Test incident response plans
controls.description: Incident response plans should be tested at least annually through exercises. Exercises should involve the board or senior leadership and cover scenarios including ransomware, data breach, and supply chain compromise.
controls.description_nl: Incident response plans should be tested at least annually through exercises. Exercises should involve the board or senior leadership and cover scenarios including ransomware, data breach, and supply chain compromise.
controls.implementation_guidance: Conduct annual tabletop exercises with senior leadership. Cover ransomware, data breach, and supply chain scenarios. Document lessons learned and update plans.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify exercise schedule and completion. Review lessons learned documentation.

controls.title: Assurance and oversight
controls.description: Directors should ensure appropriate assurance activities are in place to verify that cyber security measures are effective. This includes regular assessment, audit, and reporting to the board.
controls.description_nl: Directors should ensure appropriate assurance activities are in place to verify that cyber security measures are effective. This includes regular assessment, audit, and reporting to the board.
controls.implementation_guidance: Implement regular cyber security assessments (penetration testing, audits, vulnerability scanning). Report assurance findings to the board. Track remediation of identified issues.
controls.verification_guidance: Review assurance programme. Verify board reporting of findings. Check remediation tracking.

controls.title: Supply chain assurance
controls.description: The board should ensure that supply chain cyber risks are assessed and managed. Key suppliers should be required to meet minimum security standards and their compliance should be verified.
controls.description_nl: The board should ensure that supply chain cyber risks are assessed and managed. Key suppliers should be required to meet minimum security standards and their compliance should be verified.
controls.implementation_guidance: Assess supply chain cyber risks. Require key suppliers to hold Cyber Essentials or equivalent. Include security requirements in contracts. Monitor supplier compliance.
controls.verification_guidance: Review supplier security requirements. Verify certification evidence. Check contract security clauses.
